evidence: The initial shell brief preserved newline escape sequences literally, so @goal markers did not become column-zero delimiters and produced one merged placeholder goal.
rationale: Preconditions are an explicit hard gate before implementation and need a separately schedulable durable story.

evidence: R1 global precondition audit at latest main 0e392080b03aac9a219195541372c84b6d29746b on feat/r1-recall-telemetry: HEAD equals origin/main and descends from eb4861d; Request::Recall cosine_gate/knowledge_only, Request::Ingest scope, stable anamnesis:turn_key all exist; exact proto/capture/filtered-final-hit/knowledge-only tests passed; no pre-R1 RecallEvent/PolicyStore/recall_events exists; core schema remains v11; planned event stores metrics/query_chars but no raw query/transcript/rendered context; planned policy I/O is MCP-side and outside global lock; no R1 product/docs changes exist. Baseline cargo fmt --check and clippy -p anamnesis-mcp --all-targets -- -D warnings passed. Baseline cargo test -p anamnesis-mcp FAILED: daemon_shutdown_waits_for_migration_before_releasing_default_lock could not acquire the default lock, and embedding_migration::resume_cleanup_failure_reports_preserved_verified_backup could not acquire the migration lock (database already in use). Implementation has not started.
rationale: STEP 7 requires every global precondition and constraint to pass before implementation and requires stopping/reporting on any failure.

evidence: Global prerequisites were audited before implementation. Required source fields, turn-key stability, filtered-top gate ordering, data-minimization/policy ownership/global-lock constraints, branch/base, and targeted tests passed; fmt and strict MCP clippy passed. The mandatory MCP package baseline gate failed on two lock-acquisition tests: daemon_shutdown_waits_for_migration_before_releasing_default_lock and embedding_migration::resume_cleanup_failure_reports_preserved_verified_backup. Per STEP 7, implementation was not started and no product/docs files changed.

evidence: Task 1 targeted GREEN tests, formatting, and strict MCP clippy passed, but the mandatory full cargo test -p anamnesis-mcp gate failed again in daemon::tests::daemon_shutdown_waits_for_migration_before_releasing_default_lock at daemon.rs:1114 ('daemon acquires default lock'). Per the approved plan, Task 1 is not committed and later R1 tasks are not started. This confirms the pre-existing baseline lock test is genuinely flaky rather than cleared by repetitions.
The exact test passed five isolated serial runs and two full baseline suites, then failed again during the Task 1 mandatory package gate at daemon.rs:1114 while acquiring the default lock.
rationale: The approved plan requires stopping on any failed gate; the recurrence proves an autonomous, resolvable owner-task blocker that must be fixed before R1 continues.

evidence: Stabilized the pre-existing lock regressions without production/R1 behavior changes: daemon test now uses one production DaemonBind and isolated socket; sequential migration requests use the existing bounded fail-closed lock-release probe. Exact daemon test passed 50 runs, both resume tests passed 20 runs each, ten parallel MCP suites passed, and fresh fmt/clippy/full-test/diff gates plus architect/executor reviews are clean.

artifactRefs.description: Exact lock lifecycle stress runs, repeated parallel package suites, and fresh post-cleaner package gates.

contractCoverage.obligation: Default DB lock remains held while migration is blocked and is released only after drain.
Transient teardown latency is tolerated but a true leaked lock fails closed.
The lifecycle test is isolated from process-global socket overrides and passes normal parallel suites.

surfaceEvidence.invocation: Exact daemon lifecycle test repeated 50 times serially.
Both affected resume tests repeated 20 times each serially.
Ten consecutive normal-parallel MCP package suites plus a fresh post-cleaner full gate.

adversarialCases.scenario: Try to reacquire daemon ownership while the migration provider is blocked.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: Serve loop remains alive and reacquisition is unavailable.

adversarialCases.scenario: Try to reacquire only after provider release and migration drain.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: Replacement bind succeeds after serve loop completion.

adversarialCases.scenario: A registry lock remains held beyond the one-second deadline.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: Probe panics instead of masking the leak.

adversarialCases.scenario: OS-visible release lags briefly after prior request teardown.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: Probe retries only WouldBlock and then the real migration lease is acquired.

adversarialCases.scenario: Another test mutates ANAMNESIS_SOCKET concurrently.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: Lifecycle test uses its own temp socket and remains stable.

iteration.evidence: AI slop cleaner 38-G005LockCleaner reported no blocking findings. adversarialCases.scenario: Knowledge-only filtering is bypassed or the Episodic candidate was never top before filtering.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: The unfiltered top must be the captured Episodic id and the filtered event must contain only the surviving Semantic id.

adversarialCases.scenario: Raw query text appears in the policy table or the demo stdout.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: Only query_chars and minimized metadata are retained or printed.

adversarialCases.scenario: The trigger path is skipped yet the test reports fail-open success.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: The control adds exactly one row and the post-trigger observer records exactly one insert attempt.

adversarialCases.scenario: Telemetry failure changes the core response or leaves a partial row.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: Response stays Ok and byte-identical to the restored-state control while event count remains unchanged.

adversarialCases.scenario: Deterministic evidence is represented as completed live plugin deployment evidence or quality proof.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: Docs label telemetry eligibility/exposure only and explicitly keep external UserPromptSubmit/plugin activation pending.

contractCoverage.contractRef: R1 Tasks 2 and 6 filtered-top gate contract
contractCoverage.obligation: Apply gates after scope/tag/knowledge-only filtering and persist only final eligible result ids.
Persist query character count and minimized provenance/gate metadata without raw query, transcript, or rendered context.
Keep only the newest 10,000 recall event ids in the same transaction as insertion.
Migration/open/write/query failures disable or degrade telemetry without changing core recall, hook, graph stats, or dispatch-error behavior.
Render exact gate buckets, nearest-rank p50/p90/p95, sample/null counts, two exposure ratios, and thresholds 0.80 through 0.90 without NaN or infinity.

contractCoverage.contractRef: R1 namespace serialization and global-lock constraint
contractCoverage.obligation: Run policy disk I/O after global registry release under Memory then PolicyStore order while unrelated namespaces remain live.
Pass workspace, doctest, rustdoc, formatting, clippy, and live deterministic gates while keeping installed-plugin external activation explicitly pending.

surfaceEvidence.contractRef: R1 local production hook and forced-write-failure surface
surfaceEvidence.invocation: Fail-closed operations runbook using target/debug/anamnesis 0.19.0, embedded setup, real hook daemon path, lock probes, snapshot restoration, and SQLite trigger
anamnesis stats --recall --namespace r1-rollout --embedded
cargo test --workspace -q plus workspace doctest and rustdoc warning gates

adversarialCases.scenario: A higher-ranked captured Episodic competes with its Semantic survivor under knowledge-only recall.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: Raw top remains Episodic, while the persisted and rendered filtered top is the Semantic id.

adversarialCases.scenario: Both readout and cosine thresholds reject the final filtered hit.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: Both failure booleans remain observable, eligibility is false, and result ids are empty.

adversarialCases.scenario: Filtering removes every hit.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: The event is empty rather than falsely classified as one gate-specific abstention.

adversarialCases.scenario: Raw query text appears in schema, persisted rows, logs, or deterministic evidence output.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: Only query_chars and minimized metadata are retained; no raw-query column or value exists.

adversarialCases.scenario: Raw CLI, hook, or MCP tool attribution is confused or namespace/scope is non-canonical.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: Raw CLI maps to Unknown, UserPrompt/SessionStart/Tool are explicit, and persisted namespace/scope are canonical request provenance.

adversarialCases.scenario: A 10,001st event is inserted.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: The transaction retains exactly the newest 10,000 ids.

adversarialCases.scenario: The test reports fail-open without executing the trigger path.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: Exactly one post-trigger insert attempt is observed and no event row is added.

adversarialCases.scenario: Telemetry failure changes hook/recall bytes or leaves partial state.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: Healthy response bytes remain identical and failed transaction row count remains unchanged.

adversarialCases.scenario: The v1 version write fails or a future schema is encountered.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: Policy state disables with actionable SQLite/version evidence while core recall and counters survive.

adversarialCases.scenario: Persisted cosine data cannot be decoded during stats aggregation.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: Graph stats and usage remain byte-stable, telemetry reports unavailable, and rows remain unchanged.

adversarialCases.scenario: NaN or positive/negative infinity reaches the cosine sample boundary.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: Policy statistics reject the value rather than rendering it as missing or NaN/inf.

adversarialCases.scenario: No events or denominators exist.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: Percentiles are N/A, ratios are N/A (0/0), and all eleven sweep points render without NaN/inf.

adversarialCases.scenario: A manufactured enum order diverges from SQLite ORDER BY event_kind.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: Exact renderer fixture follows production SessionStart, Tool, Unknown, UserPrompt order.

adversarialCases.scenario: Policy I/O occurs under the global registry lock or outside the corresponding Memory lock.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: Policy I/O observes registry release and Memory then Policy order; another namespace completes independently.

adversarialCases.scenario: Local deterministic hook evidence is represented as installed-plugin external activation or answer quality proof.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: Docs label eligibility/exposure only and keep real external activation/reactivation pending.

adversarialCases.scenario: Review or test receipts refer to a different source revision or omit doc/rustdoc gates.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: Artifacts are pinned to 3ca0f37 and include exact workspace, doctest, rustdoc, clippy, format, and diff evidence.

Final architect agents 82-85 and executor QA agent 87 then reviewed the same frozen revision with zero blockers.

evidence: STEP 12 Part 2 completed via merged PR #135 at 02b0b2d. Ten internal runs measured client-zero-to-grace at 1003/1004/1004ms min/median/max and teardown at 0-1ms. Twenty external runs measured post-adapter-reap-to-unlink at 2328/2353/2380ms. Correlated residual about 1345ms is consistent with adapter-close to daemon EOF/client-zero handoff. Existing 20s timeout retained, adapter reap bounded, all local gates and CI passed. Structured receipt: /tmp/step12-g011-receipt.json.

architectReview.evidence: Final frozen review found truthful shutdown phase boundaries, unchanged evidence-backed timeout, bounded adapter reap, no R3 behavior, and no remaining findings.

executorQa.evidence: Real two-launcher MCP E2E, reconnect cancellation, zero-grace startup, blocked migration drain, direct final-close-to-unlink timing, serialized workspace suite, and final CI all passed.

artifactRefs.description: Merged PR, full gates, CI, phase measurements, and evidence-backed timeout conclusion.

contractCoverage.obligation: Measure daemon grace, client detection, teardown, and socket unlink boundaries and make no guessed timeout increase.

surfaceEvidence.contractRef: two launchers share one daemon then grace-exit
surfaceEvidence.invocation: cargo test -p anamnesis-mcp --test multi_client_daemon two_launchers_share_one_daemon_then_grace_exits -- --exact --nocapture
cargo test --workspace -- --test-threads=1

adversarialCases.scenario: A client reconnects after a real 1-to-0 transition while the original grace deadline is armed.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: The original deadline is canceled and a fresh full grace starts only after the reconnect leaves.

adversarialCases.scenario: A migration worker remains blocked while the final client disconnects.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: The daemon retains ownership until drain completes and the measured drain includes the blocked interval.

adversarialCases.scenario: A serve adapter does not exit after stdin EOF.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: The harness kills and reaps it at the existing 20s allowance and fails with diagnostics instead of hanging.
